target.name: handoff
target.source_summary: templates/handoff.md + commands/handoff.md + Delivery Constitution rule 5 (missing evidence should remain visible) + per-agent Handoff sections

gates.description: A requested next action exists and names the receiving role's first concrete step. A handoff without one dead-ends the pipeline.

dimensions.description: The complete/in-progress split matches the evidence record exactly.

anchors.1: 'Mostly done' vagueness, or items claimed complete that the evidence contradicts.
anchors.3: Split present and largely accurate, but at least one in-progress item reads as complete.
anchors.5: Every item's status matches judgments and evidence; nothing overstated.

dimensions.description: Active assumptions are listed and distinguishable from facts.

anchors.1: No assumptions listed although the work visibly made inferences.
anchors.3: Assumptions listed but interleaved with facts, or missing how to verify them.
anchors.5: Every active assumption listed, marked as assumption, with how the receiver could verify it.

dimensions.description: What still needs verification or decision remains visible (rule 5).

anchors.1: Risks/unknowns section empty while caps, tier-ceiling items, or missing verification exist in the record.
anchors.3: Risks named generically without what would close them.
anchors.5: Each unknown named with the specific verification or decision that would close it.

anchors.3: Evidence mentioned ('tests passed') but not linked to runs/logs/artifacts.
anchors.5: Tests, logs, and inspections cited per claim — the receiver can re-derive trust without re-doing work.

anchors.3: Partial list — misses files present in artifact metadata.
anchors.5: Matches the artifact record exactly, including config and schema surfaces.

aggregation.gate_handling: Apply gate caps after normalization; lowest applicable cap wins.

weight_rationale: state_fidelity is heaviest because a handoff exists to transfer trust between roles — an overstated completion status transmits false trust, which is worse than no handoff at all.

known_good.content: From: engineer. To: tester. Complete: Worker login endpoint (T2) — see implementation note IN-9, run_code evidence ev-201..204. In progress: none. Not started: rate-limit tuning (T2 risk). Files: workers/auth.js, src/utils/auth.js, src/utils/crypto.js, migrations/0007_sessions.sql. Assumptions: 30-day session expiry (verify against product if it matters). Risks/unknowns: concurrent session cap unverified — needs a concurrency harness; fixed-window limiter allows 2x burst at window edge. Evidence: ev-201 (200 path), ev-202 (401), ev-203 (429 body fields), ev-204 (module load). Next action: tester writes API tests for the four covered paths plus the concurrency gap flagged above.

known_bad.content: From: engineer. To: tester. Current state: login is basically done and working. Files: the auth stuff. Assumptions: none. Risks: none really. Evidence: tested it locally, all good. Next action: whatever's next.
